meaning: adj.缺乏的,不足的,稀疏的,俭省的,不充足的
examples: The boy followed the old man, who commanded him to open the bag in which he carried his scanty dinner.
Details of his life are scanty.
So far, what scanty evidence we have points to two suspects.
TSE, and found that most had started late and provided only scanty details of their progress.
The scanty winter rainfall is brought by Mediterranean depressions, but the amounts are rather variable from year to year.
